exercises.title: Binary Data with struct
exercises.description: Use the struct module to pack and unpack binary data. Pack student records (name length, GPA as float, age as short int) and unpack them back. Print each field.
exercises.starterCode:
import struct

# Pack format: 'H' = unsigned short (2 bytes), 'f' = float (4 bytes)
# Big-endian format prefix '>'

records = [
    (20, 3.9),   # (age, gpa)
    (22, 3.5),
    (21, 3.7),
]

# TODO: For each record, pack (age, gpa) using struct.pack('>Hf', age, gpa)
# Collect packed bytes in a list

# TODO: Print the size of one packed record
# Format: "Record size: 6 bytes"

# TODO: Unpack each packed record and print
# Format: "Age: 20, GPA: 3.90"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Record size: 6 bytes
Age: 20, GPA: 3.90
Age: 22, GPA: 3.50
Age: 21, GPA: 3.70

exercises.title: Pickle: Serialize and Deserialize Objects
exercises.description: Use pickle to serialize a list of custom objects to bytes (in-memory) and deserialize them back. Verify the data is identical after the round trip.
exercises.starterCode:
import pickle

class Student:
    def __init__(self, name, gpa, courses):
        self.name = name
        self.gpa = gpa
        self.courses = courses

    def __eq__(self, other):
        return (self.name == other.name and
                self.gpa == other.gpa and
                self.courses == other.courses)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Student({self.name}, {self.gpa})"


students = [
    Student("Alice", 3.9, ["Python", "Math"]),
    Student("Bob", 3.5, ["History", "Art"]),
    Student("Carol", 3.7, ["Physics", "Chemistry", "Math"]),
]

# TODO: Serialize students list to bytes using pickle.dumps()
# TODO: Print the size of the serialized bytes
# Format: "Serialized size: N bytes"

# TODO: Deserialize back using pickle.loads()
# TODO: Print whether original == restored
# Format: "Data preserved: True"

# TODO: Print each restored student's name and GPA
# Format: "Alice: GPA 3.90, 2 courses"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Serialized size: 219 bytes
Data preserved: True
Alice: GPA 3.90, 2 courses
Bob: GPA 3.50, 2 courses
Carol: GPA 3.70, 3 courses
hints: for s in restored: print(f'{s.name}: GPA {s.gpa:.2f}, {len(s.courses)} courses')

exercises.title: configparser: Parse and Write Config
exercises.description: Use configparser to parse an INI-style config string and extract typed values. Then build a new config and serialize it to a string.
exercises.starterCode:
import configparser
import io

# Simulate reading from a config file
config_text = """
[database]
host = localhost
port = 5432
name = myapp_db
max_connections = 10

[app]
debug = false
log_level = INFO
max_upload_mb = 16.5

[cache]
enabled = true
ttl_seconds = 300
"""

config = configparser.ConfigParser()
config.read_string(config_text)

# TODO: Read and print database.host and database.port (as int)
# Format: "DB: localhost:5432"

# TODO: Read and print app.debug (as bool) and app.max_upload_mb (as float)
# Format: "Debug: False, Max upload: 16.5 MB"

# TODO: Read and print cache.enabled (as bool) and cache.ttl_seconds (as int)
# Format: "Cache: enabled=True, ttl=300s"

# TODO: Print all sections
# Format: "Sections: ['database', 'app', 'cache']"
exercises.expectedOutput:
DB: localhost:5432
Debug: False, Max upload: 16.5 MB
Cache: enabled=True, ttl=300s
Sections: ['database', 'app', 'cache']
hints: config.sections() returns list of section names

warmup.title: Quick Recall: struct pack/unpack
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace the struct pack and unpack operations.
warmup.starterCode:
import struct

packed = struct.pack('>iH', 1000, 42)
print(len(packed))
unpacked = struct.unpack('>iH', packed)
print(unpacked)
print(unpacked[0])
